rspStmt.AuthEnty: National Economic Development and Labour Council (NEDLAC)

prodStmt.copyright: Copyright, National Economic Development and Labour Council (NEDLAC)

serStmt.serInfo: National Economic Development and Labour Council (NEDLAC). Infrastructure Delivery in South Africa,1998 [dataset]. Pretoria: NEDLAC[producer], 2000. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2015. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/h18p-8v81

verStmt.version: v1: Edited, anonymised data for public distribution

stdyInfo.abstract:
In November 1998, the Development Chamber of National Economic Development and Labour Council (NEDLAC) contracted the Community Agency for Social Enquiry (CASE) and the Norwegian Institute for Applied Social Science (FAFO) to undertake a project on infrastructure delivery. The project had three phases: The first phase identified the extent of government's infrastructure expenditure. The second phase of the project focused on the organisation of infrastructure delivery. This analysis identified strengths and weaknesses within processes of infrastructure delivery.
The third phase of the project was a 2 600 household survey in the provinces of Gauteng, KwaZulu- Natal and the Northern Province. The survey, conducted by CASE, assessed the extent of delivery as well as recipients' perceptions of the quality of infrastructure delivery.

sumDscr.geogCover: Gauteng, KwaZulu-Natal and the Northern Province.
sumDscr.geogUnit: The lowest level of geographic aggregation for the data is province - Gauteng, KwaZulu-Natal and the Northern Province.
sumDscr.universe: The survey covered all household members in South Africa

stdyInfo.notes: Questions in the survey were concerned with the employment status and income of households, electricity supply, telephones, sanitation and refuse removal, education, housing, health services, social security and community developments. Background data was collected on age, gender, educational level, employment status, race, province, marital status, area and type of dwelling .

dataColl.dataCollector: Fafo Institute for Applied Social Science
dataColl.sampProc: The sampling design specified 1000 respondents in each of Gauteng, KwaZulu Natal and the Northern Province. Within each province the sample was stratified by population group (African, White, Coloured and Indian) and area (urban or rural).
dataColl.collSitu: The fieldwork was undertaken by the fieldwork department of the Community Agency on Social Enquiry (CASE). Within each province 250 enumerator areas were randomly selected with a probability proportional to size, stratified by race and area. Within each EA 4 households were randomly selected for interviewing. A senior person in the household was asked to give information about the entire household. An adult older than 20 was then randomly selected to answer a more detailed set of questions. If the person selected to be interviewed was not available to answer questions the fieldworker would make two further attempts to complete the interview. If the household was not available, or was unwilling to be interviewed another household would be substituted to be interviewed.
dataColl.weight: The data was weighted to reflect the racial distribution of the provinces surveyed. The sample was over or under weighted to reflect the number of Africans, Coloureds, Indians or Whites interviewed in relation to the actual percentage of these population groups.

useStmt.citReq: National Economic Development and Labour Council (NEDLAC). Infrastructure Delivery in South Africa,1998 [dataset]. Pretoria: NEDLAC[producer], 2000. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2015. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/h18p-8v81
useStmt.conditions: Public use files, available to all

var.name: q1
var.labl: number of person selected from the grid

var.name: q2
var.labl: total number of members in household

catgry.labl: rural : housing on a commercial farm
more than 1 year but less than 2 years
more than 6 months but less than 1 year
supported by persons in the houshold
supported by relatives not in the household

var.name: q19
var.labl: are you looking for work for pay?

catgry.labl: already have a job, but have not yet started working
do not have the necessary skills
other persons in the house earn enough money
parent/guardian will not allow me to work (forbidden to work
spuse/partner will not allow me to work
taking care of home on full-time basis

var.name: q24
var.labl: do you receive elec in dwelling?

var.name: q26
var.labl: noticed new elec services in last 5 yrs?

catgry.labl: no, there have been no new projects
yes, in both residences and streets

var.name: q27
var.labl: are elec services wrk and maintained?

catgry.labl: no, regular cut-offs in residences and streets

var.name: q27a
var.labl: how long to rep. elec services?

var.name: q29
var.labl: noticed new pub phone services in last 5 yrs?

catgry.labl: no, they are frequently out of order
it is removed by the municipality
there is a communal refuse dump
we have a private rubbish collection service
we have our own refuse dump

var.name: q33
var.labl: what toilet facility does hhld use?

var.name: q34
var.labl: noticed new sanit or sewerage services in last 5 yrs?

var.name: q35
var.labl: are san and sew working properly?

catgry.labl: no, they are frequently overflowing or blocked
no, they often smell bad and are a health hazard

var.name: q35a
var.labl: how long to repair san and sew?

var.name: q37
var.labl: noticed new bldng of schools or upgrading in last 5 yrs?

var.labl: how many kids under 18 live with you?

var.name: q39
var.labl: do one or more of kids go to school?

teachers attitudes and perception of pupils
teachers lack of commitment to teaching
too many pupils in the class

var.name: q46
var.labl: main reason why not complete matric

catgry.labl: caring for relatives (elderly, children, ill, etc)
concerned about safety travelling to school
education should be free for all
school fees should be according to income

var.name: q51
var.labl: who owns land where dwelling is?

var.labl: noticed new housing projs to improv inf. settlement in last 5 yrs?

var.labl: main source of water for hhld

less than quarter of an hour
more than 15 minutes, but less than 30 minutes
